applyFeed(feed, options).purpose: Render a validated feed and throw on invalid input.

setFeed(feed, options).purpose: Render a validated feed and throw on invalid input.

trySetFeed(feed, options).returns: { ok: true, state } or { ok: false, error }

reload().purpose: Fetch and render the configured public feed endpoint.

refresh().purpose: Refresh the runtime feed from configured endpoint or mock input.

connectEvents().purpose: Connect the EventSource status-feed/events stream when configured.

syncRoute().purpose: Synchronize selected bar or incident panel with location.hash.

clearInteractionState().purpose: Clear hover, touch pin, selected, tooltip, and transient interaction state.

rerender().purpose: Re-render the current feed without changing the feed source.

getState().purpose: Expose runtime diagnostics without leaking secrets.

getFeed().purpose: Return the current public feed object.

destroy().purpose: Abort listeners, timers, EventSource, and animation frames.

yonerai-status-runtime-ready.behavior: Emitted after the runtime globals are installed.

invariants: Do not write status DOM directly from AWS, YonerAI API, cron, or admin tooling.
Only yonerai.status.feed.v1 reaches the browser renderer.
healthcheck, monitor, and source inputs must be converted before runtime rendering.
Invalid feed updates must not leave stale selected highlights, duplicate panels, or duplicate tooltip nodes.
Feed updates must preserve hover, touch, animation, and selected-state contracts unless clearInteractionState() is explicitly called.
Secrets and raw internal endpoint inventories must never be included in public feed JSON.
Public status claims must be represented as feed data and must not imply production operation before live monitoring is connected.
